event: View of Old Saint Peter's Basilica and the Vatican Palace
symbols: Saint Peter's Basilica — the church built over the traditional site of the apostle Peter's burial, the seat of Western Christendom
Vatican Palace — the temporal and spiritual authority of the papacy
monumental staircase — the ascent to sacred space and ecclesiastical power
fortified walls — the Church as fortress and refuge
description: A wide, horizontal pen-and-ink drawing depicting a panoramic view of a large architectural complex on a hillside. At left, a grand basilica with a prominent bell tower and classical facade rises above a monumental staircase leading up from a lower level. To the right, a massive multi-story palace building extends along the ridge, its walls punctuated by rows of windows. Fortification walls and smaller structures fill the spaces between. Small figures and what appear to be horses and carts populate the foreground area at the base of the complex.

_meta.title: Rome, old Saint Peter's Basilica and the Vatican Palace
